questions.title: 1. Push Ups - x10 Reps (Full Range)
questions.instructions: Perform 10 full push-ups (chest to floor, arms fully locked out). Keep control of your shoulder blades, no winging or collapsing.

options.label: A
options.text: I did all 10 reps with great control and full range.

options.label: B
options.text: I did all 10, but struggled with depth or shoulder stability and had to drop to my knees.

options.label: C
options.text: I couldn't complete 10 reps or had shoulder/elbow discomfort.

questions.title: 2. Single-Leg RDL (Romanian Deadlift)
questions.instructions: Stand on one leg. Hinge at the hips, extend the other leg behind you, and keep your torso flat as you lower, then return to standing. Repeat each side.
questions.prompt: What did your balance and control feel like?

options.label: A
options.text: I moved with balance and control, hips stayed square.

options.label: B
options.text: I wobbled a bit or twisted my hips but completed the movement.

options.label: C
options.text: I lost balance or couldn't control the movement well.

questions.title: 3. Passive Hang - 20 Seconds with Scapular Depression
questions.instructions: Hang from a bar with arms straight, shoulders pulled slightly down (not shrugged to ears). Hold for 20 seconds.

options.label: A
options.text: I held for 20 seconds with scapular depression and no pain.

options.label: B
options.text: I held it but struggled to keep shoulders down or started to slip.

options.label: C
options.text: I couldn't hold for 20 seconds or had shoulder/wrist pain.

questions.title: 4. Bear Crawl - 20 Seconds with Knees Hovering
questions.instructions: Hands and toes on the ground, knees off the floor. Crawl forward slowly while keeping hips steady and back flat.
questions.prompt: How did your bear crawl go?

options.label: A
options.text: I moved smoothly with hips level and core tight.

options.label: B
options.text: I completed it but my hips wobbled or back arched.

options.label: C
options.text: I struggled to stay in position or had joint pain/discomfort.

questions.title: 5. Crow Hold or Handstand Kick-Up (Balance Awareness)
questions.instructions: Attempt a crow hold OR a kick into a handstand. Focus on balance and body awareness, it is not about holding for long, just control and intention.
questions.prompt: What best describes your skill level?

options.label: A
options.text: I balanced in Crow / Handstand or felt in control during my attempt.

options.label: B
options.text: I entered Crow / Handstand but was wobbly or uncertain.

options.label: C
options.text: I couldn't get into position or felt unstable and unsure.

questions.title: 6. Pigeon Stretch - 30s Each Side (No Knee or Lower Back Pain)
questions.instructions: Get into a pigeon stretch. Fold over your front leg and breathe. Stay here for 30 seconds per side.

options.label: A
options.text: I felt a deep hip stretch with no discomfort.

options.label: B
options.text: I felt some tension or had to adjust but managed.

options.label: C
options.text: I felt pain or couldn't get into the stretch properly.

pass.title:
🥳 🎉 🥳 🎉 Congratulations 🥳 🎉 🥳 🎉

✅ AMS | Prime™ - PASSED!
pass.body:
Great stuff. You've passed the Prime Assessment. That means you've built enough foundation to take on the next level of strength, control, and mobility.

You've topped out the assessment ladder so far. Stay consistent, keep refining the basics, and check back when Rise + Peak go live.

fail.title: 🟢 Recommended Level: AMS | Prime™
fail.body:
Your movement assessment shows that AMS | Prime™ is the right level for you. Aim to challenge yourself through the program.

This phase is about laying the right groundwork: building balance, control, and the foundational strength you can rely on as you progress up the chain.
